<findings>

[suggestion] [warning] Zone "PRD Structural Validation" (packages-rex:prd-structural-validation) has critical risk (score: 0.64, cohesion: 0.36, coupling: 0.64) — requires refactoring before new feature development

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.36) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.64) — 17 imports target "prd-domain-operations"
- epic-correlation.ts and structural.ts are general-purpose core utilities that appear in this zone due to test co-location — evaluate whether they belong in the core PRD domain zone instead
- The interactive validate path (validate-interactive.ts) combines user-facing I/O with validation logic; splitting presentation from validation rules would make the rules unit-testable in isolation
- Three validate test files land here alongside production code, which inflates the zone's apparent scope — the test gravity is a signal that validate logic is tightly coupled to its own tests rather than shared domain infrastructure
- Cohesion 0.36 and coupling 0.64 both exceed warning thresholds; the zone conflates core utility files, CLI command handlers, and test files into one community.
- validate → core carries 17 imports, confirming that validation heavily depends on domain primitives; if epic-correlation.ts and structural.ts moved to the core zone this coupling score would drop significantly.
- Zone "prd-structural-validation" has files across 5 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- The 17 validate→core imports with only 1 reverse import suggests epic-correlation.ts and structural.ts are defined inside the validate zone but heavily referenced back toward core consumers — if these utilities were pinned to core the coupling arrow would reverse direction, which is the correct orientation for domain utilities.
- epic-correlation.ts and structural.ts appear to originate in prd-structural-validation but flow 17 imports upward into prd-domain-operations; a utility that is more heavily consumed by core than by its own zone has inverted dependency direction — moving these files to core would align the import arrow with the architectural hierarchy.
- tests/integration/smart-add-orphaned-parent.test.ts lands in this zone while all other integration tests live in tests/integration/ without a dedicated zone — the integration test directory has 5 files but no cohesive zone, meaning integration test coverage is invisible to zone health reporting
- epic-correlation.ts imports from ../analyze/dedupe.js, creating a transitive dependency from the validation zone into the analyze subsystem that is invisible in the direct zone-coupling metrics — the validate→core coupling count understates the true fan-out
- epic-correlation.ts imports from src/analyze/dedupe.js — a core utility file pulling in an analyze-subsystem dependency creates a hidden transitive coupling path from validation → core → analyze that bypasses zone boundary enforcement
- One integration test (smart-add-orphaned-parent.test.ts) is stranded in the structural-validation zone while four other integration tests in the same directory have no zone assignment — the integration test layer lacks a dedicated zone, making cross-integration-test dependencies invisible to zone health tooling
- validate-interactive.ts mixes terminal I/O (readline prompts) with validation orchestration in a single file — the interactive presentation logic can change independently of the validation rules, but there is no seam between the two; extracting a validate-presenter.ts for I/O would make validate rules unit-testable without a TTY
- The three unit test files (validate.test.ts, validate-interactive.test.ts, validate-epicless.test.ts) test three distinct behaviors but live in a single zone alongside production code — there is no test file for epic-correlation.ts or structural.ts, meaning the most-coupled utilities in the zone have no dedicated tests visible in zone health reporting
- Extract terminal I/O from validate-interactive.ts into a validate-presenter.ts module to create a seam between validation rules and presentation — rules become unit-testable without TTY and the presentation layer can be swapped for future non-interactive modes
- RISK AREA: cohesion 0.36 + coupling 0.64 both at warning thresholds — the zone simultaneously owns core utilities (epic-correlation, structural), CLI command handlers (validate, validate-interactive), and three unit test files; a single change to the validate command interface requires updating files from all three categories
- [call graph] 466 internal calls, 75 outgoing, 3 incoming (cohesion: 0.86, coupling: 0.14)

</insights>
